meta.title: Wind Turbine Work at Height + Rescue Plan
meta.subtitle: {turbine} / {site} — {WAH activity}
meta.author: HSE Renewables
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label and GWO competence is cited as a requirement (certificate numbers are not reproduced). Identifiers were pseudonymised before drafting; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: This plan governs work at height on {turbine} at {site} for {WAH activity}. Control is led by the Work at Height Regulations 2005 reg-6 hierarchy (avoid -> prevent, collective before personal -> mitigate/arrest), and a MANDATORY tested rescue plan recovers a suspended worker within minutes by the team's own two-person-minimum capability. 'Call 999 / wait for emergency services' is NOT the rescue plan.

rows.control: AVOID work at height — {avoidance option e.g. lower the component / ground-level work / man-rider}
PREVENT a fall — collective protection first ({guardrail / working platform})
MITIGATE / ARREST — personal fall-arrest on a rated anchor (only where avoidance + collective not practicable)

rows: Team-owned descent/haul kit + GWO ART method
Supplement only — called in parallel, NOT the rescue plan
sections.caption: Rescue plan — tested, team-owned, recovery within minutes; 999 is a supplement only

sections.text: A WAH plan with no tested rescue is rejected (reg 4). 'Call the emergency services and wait' is NOT a rescue plan — suspension trauma can be fatal within minutes.
Weather hold / stop thresholds (owned by REN-03)

items: Hub-height wind cut-off ~= 15 m/s [ASSUMED — confirm with weather-dynamic-risk-assessment (REN-03); proposed-and-user-confirmed, not a citation]
Lightning stand-down per NFPA 780 practice / a lightning-warning service [GAP — site-confirmed]
BS 7121-1 (2016) man-riding ceiling 7 m/s [VERIFIED] where a personnel-carrier lift is used

sections.text: The climb team holds current GWO Working at Heights / First Aid / Advanced Rescue Training competence, refreshed every 2 years. Certificate numbers are a licensed competence-scheme identifier and are NOT reproduced here ([GAP]); competence is held in a separate access-controlled training record.

items: GWO Working at Heights / First Aid / Advanced Rescue Training (competence requirement)
